UAE Corporate Tax Services

UAE Corporate Tax (9% Regime)

Corporate tax compliance including registration, filing, and strategic tax planning. Our tax specialists ensure compliance with the new UAE corporate tax regime while optimizing your position.

Get a QuoteBook Consultation

Understanding UAE Corporate Tax

The UAE introduced federal corporate tax effective from financial years starting on or after 1 June 2023. The standard rate is 9% on taxable profits above AED 375,000. Small businesses with profits below AED 375,000 pay 0% tax.

Free zone qualifying income may be taxed at 0% if certain conditions are met. Our team helps navigate these complexities and ensures your business benefits from available exemptions and reliefs.

Corporate Tax Rates

Profits up to AED 375,0000%
Profits above AED 375,0009%
Qualifying Free Zone Income0%

*Rates as of 2023 for financial years starting on or after 1 June 2023. Subject to change.

Our Corporate Tax Services

Corporate Tax Registration

Complete registration process including threshold assessment, application submission, and tax ID issuance.

Tax Computation

Detailed corporate tax calculations including adjustments, reliefs, and free zone qualifying income.

Tax Planning

Proactive tax planning to optimize your position including free zone structuring and group optimization.

Free Zone Qualification

Assessment and documentation for qualifying free zone income to benefit from 0% tax rate.

Compliance Filing

Preparation and filing of corporate tax returns with complete supporting documentation.

Audit Support

Full support during FTA corporate tax audits including representation and documentation.

Free Zone Income Qualification

Free zone persons may benefit from 0% corporate tax on qualifying income if they meet certain conditions including maintaining adequate assets in the free zone, deriving qualifying income, and meeting de minimis requirements.

Qualifying Income Includes

  • • Transactions with other free zone persons
  • • Qualifying income from non-free zone persons
  • • Certain income from related parties
  • • Income from carrying on qualifying activities

Requirements

  • • Maintain adequate assets in free zone
  • • Derive qualifying income
  • • Meet de minimis requirements
  • • Maintain proper documentation

Corporate Tax Timeline

01

Registration

Register for corporate tax within 3 months of exceeding threshold or financial year start.

02

Record Keeping

Maintain financial records for at least 7 years from tax year end.

03

Tax Calculation

Calculate taxable income and tax liability with all applicable reliefs.

04

Filing & Payment

File corporate tax return and pay tax within 9 months of financial year end.

Frequently Asked Questions

Who must register for corporate tax?

All UAE businesses and individuals carrying on business in the UAE must register if they meet the registration threshold or have taxable income. Registration is required within 3 months of the financial year start or exceeding the threshold.

When is corporate tax due?

Corporate tax returns must be filed within 9 months of the financial year end. Tax payment is due by the same date. We track all deadlines and ensure timely filing.

What is the difference between mainland and free zone tax?

Mainland businesses pay 9% on profits above AED 375,000. Free zone businesses may pay 0% on qualifying income if they meet specific conditions. Non-qualifying income is taxed at 9% regardless of location.

Can foreign tax be credited against UAE tax?

Yes. UAE has a foreign tax credit system allowing credit for foreign tax paid on income that is also taxable in the UAE. This prevents double taxation on foreign-sourced income.

Navigate UAE Corporate Tax with Confidence

Get expert corporate tax advice and ensure full compliance with the new regime.
